evidence.scientific_clarity: All three equations (ELBO, decomposition, retrieval-augmented variant) are present with correct notation and connecting prose that explains each derivation step.
evidence.typography: PDF uses Type 1 Computer Modern fonts (CMBX12, CMR10, CMMI10, MSBM10, etc.) embedded as subsets — proper LaTeX math typography with italic variables and upright operators.
evidence.palette: Text-only derivation block; no color used, so no colorblind or directional-palette issues possible.
evidence.layout_density: Single-page layout is functional but somewhat sparse; the final equation (3) sits in generous whitespace at the bottom with no further prose filling the page.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption uses \eqref cross-references matching the equations, but the caption contains no quantitative/numerical finding — it only restates what each equation is, which a reviewer would flag.
evidence.vector_fidelity: pdffonts confirms all fonts are Type 1 (not rasterized), embedded, subsetted, with Unicode mapping — text is fully selectable (verified via pdftotext).
evidence.file_format: figure.pdf and figure.png and caption.tex and gen_figure.py are all present and non-trivial; equations.tex (required by brief) is absent from the deliverables directory.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption explains what each equation is and names the contribution, but provides no numerical claim or specific finding (e.g., no bound tightness value, no improvement figure) — purely descriptive.

overall_strengths: Mathematically rigorous: correct LaTeX environments (align, \eqref, \mathbb{E}, \mathrm{KL}, \mathcal{L}) with proper equation numbering and inter-equation prose explaining each derivation step.
True vector PDF with fully embedded, selectable Computer Modern Type 1 fonts — print-ready and editable.
overall_weaknesses: equations.tex source file is missing from the output directory despite the brief requiring it as a deliverable, and gen_figure.py references it as the primary input.
Caption is purely descriptive with no quantitative finding or claim; a reviewer would ask what the figure demonstrates beyond a textbook derivation chain.
